name: Lomo Saltado
steps: Sear the beef 3-4 minutes.
Add onion, stir-fry 3 minutes.
Toss with roasted potato fries.

ingredients_full.note: cut into fries and roasted or fried until crisp

steps_detailed: Roast or fry the potato fries until golden and crisp, set aside.
Heat a splash of oil in a wok or wide skillet over very high heat. Sear the beef in a single layer, 2-3 minutes per side, until well-browned. Remove.
Add the onion and garlic to the same pan, stir-frying 2-3 minutes until just softened but still with some bite.
Add the tomato, soy sauce, and vinegar - this soy-vinegar combination is the signature of lomo saltado, reflecting Peru's Chinese-Cantonese immigrant influence (chifa cuisine).
Return the beef to the pan and toss everything together over high heat for 1-2 minutes.
Fold in the crisp potato fries right at the end so they stay crunchy, and top with cilantro.

name: Aji de Gallina
steps: Simmer chicken with aji paste and milk, 10 minutes, until creamy.
Serve over cooked rice.
steps_detailed: Soak the torn bread in the milk until soft, then blend or mash it into the milk until smooth - this is the traditional thickener for the creamy sauce.
Heat a splash of oil in a pan over medium heat. Cook the onion and garlic 5-6 minutes until softened.
Stir in the aji amarillo paste, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the milk-bread mixture and Parmesan, stirring until it forms a smooth, thick, pale-orange sauce.
Fold in the shredded chicken and simmer 8-10 minutes, stirring, until heated through and creamy.
Cook the rice per package directions. Serve the chicken over rice, garnished with sliced hard-boiled egg and olives.

name: Arroz con Pollo (Peruvian)
steps: Brown the chicken, 5-6 minutes.
Add rice, cilantro, and 1 cup water, cook 20 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, plus more for serving

steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium-high heat and brown the chicken, 5-6 minutes. Remove.
Add the onion and garlic, cooking 5 minutes until softened.
Blend or finely chop the cilantro with a splash of the broth, then add to the pot along with the aji amarillo paste - this is what gives arroz con pollo its distinctive green color.
Stir in the rice, coating it in the green sauce, then add the remaining broth and corn.
Nestle the chicken back in, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and cook 20-25 minutes until the rice is tender.
Top with extra fresh cilantro before serving.

name: Seco de Res (Peruvian Beef Stew)
steps: Brown the beef, 5-6 minutes.
Add cilantro and potato with 3/4 cup water, simmer 30 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, plus more for serving

steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a heavy pot over medium-high heat and brown the beef, 5-6 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic, cooking 5 minutes until softened.
Blend the cilantro with the broth until smooth, then add to the pot with the aji amarillo paste - the cilantro-forward sauce is what makes this dish 'seco' (green).
Add the potato and corn. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 30-35 minutes until the beef and potato are tender.
Top with extra cilantro before serving.

name: Grilled Anticuchos
steps: Marinate beef in aji paste and vinegar 15 minutes.
Skewer and grill 3-4 minutes per side.
steps_detailed: Whisk the aji amarillo paste, vinegar, garlic, cumin, and oil together into a marinade.
Coat the beef cubes and marinate at least 20 minutes, or up to overnight for deeper flavor.
Thread onto skewers.

Grill over high heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until charred outside and cooked to preference - anticuchos are a beloved Peruvian street food, traditionally made with beef heart, though this version uses steak for easier everyday cooking.

name: Peruvian Fish Stew (Chupe-Style)
steps: Simmer potato in 1 cup water 10 minutes.
Add fish and milk, simmer 10-12 minutes until fish is cooked.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium heat. Cook the onion and garlic 4-5 minutes.
Stir in the aji amarillo paste, then add 1 cup water and the potato. Simmer 10 minutes.
Add the corn and milk, and nestle the fish into the pot.
Simmer 10-12 minutes until the fish flakes easily and the potato is tender.
Crack the egg directly into the simmering stew in the last few minutes to poach, a traditional chupe touch.
Top with cilantro before serving.

name: Peruvian Chicken and Rice
steps: Rub chicken with aji paste, roast or pan-sear until cooked through.
Serve over cooked rice.
steps_detailed: Whisk the aji amarillo paste, lime juice, garlic, and cumin together into a marinade.
Coat the chicken and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Pan-sear or roast until cooked through (165F internal), about 18-20 minutes if roasting at 400F.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve the chicken over it, topped with cilantro.

name: Ceviche

ingredients.note: or other firm white fish, cubed

steps: Toss fish with lime juice and onion, marinate 15-20 minutes until fish turns opaque.

ingredients_full.note: or other very fresh firm white fish, cubed

steps_detailed: Use the freshest, highest-quality fish you can find, since it's 'cooked' entirely by the acid in this dish.
Toss the cubed fish with the lime juice, aji amarillo paste, and half the onion.
Marinate 15-20 minutes, just until the fish turns opaque and firms up - much longer and the texture turns chalky.
Fold in the cilantro and remaining onion right before serving.
Serve with cooked corn kernels and boiled sweet potato slices on the side, the traditional ceviche accompaniments called 'leche de tigre' garnishes.

name: Causa Rellena
steps: Mash potato with lime juice, layer with shredded chicken.
steps_detailed: Mash the warm potato with the lime juice and aji amarillo paste until smooth - the potato itself should be well-seasoned, since it's the base and top layer of the dish.
Mix the shredded chicken with mayonnaise.
Press half the potato into a small dish or ring mold, layer the chicken mixture on top, then cover with the remaining potato.
Chill at least 20 minutes to firm up.
Garnish with sliced hard-boiled egg and olives before serving - causa is traditionally served cold, almost like a savory terrine.

name: Peruvian Quinoa Salad
steps: Toss cooled quinoa with corn and onion.
steps_detailed: Whisk the lime juice and olive oil together for the dressing.
Toss the cooled quinoa with the corn, onion, and tomatoes.
Add the dressing and toss to coat evenly.
Fold in the cilantro just before serving so it stays bright.

name: Peruvian Chicken and Potato Soup
steps: Simmer potato in broth 15 minutes.
Add shredded chicken and warm through.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium heat. Cook the onion and garlic 4-5 minutes.
Stir in the aji amarillo paste, then add the broth and potato. Simmer 15-18 minutes until the potato is tender.
Add the shredded chicken and warm through, 3-4 minutes.
Top with cilantro before serving.

name: Peruvian Rice and Bean Bowl
steps: Warm the beans with onion.
Serve over cooked rice.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pan over medium heat. Cook the onion and garlic 4-5 minutes.
Add the cumin, cooking 30 seconds, then add the beans and warm through, 5-6 minutes.
Finish with lime juice.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve the beans over it, topped with cilantro.

name: Peruvian Egg and Potato Scramble
steps: Cook potato and onion 5 minutes.
Add eggs and scramble, 3-4 minutes.
steps_detailed: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook the potato and onion 6-7 minutes until the potato is tender and lightly golden.
Whisk the eggs with the aji amarillo paste.
Pour into the pan and scramble gently, 3-4 minutes, until just set.
Top with cilantro before serving.

name: Quinoa Breakfast Porridge (Peruvian)
steps: Simmer quinoa in milk 15 minutes until creamy.
Sweeten with honey.
steps_detailed: Rinse the quinoa well, then combine with the milk in a saucepan.
Bring to a simmer and cook 15-18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the quinoa is tender and the mixture has thickened.
Stir in the cinnamon and raisins, then sweeten with honey before serving warm - quinoa has been a staple grain in the Andes for thousands of years.

name: Roasted Corn Snack (Cancha)
steps: Toast corn in oil over medium-high heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes until golden and crunchy.
steps_detailed: Pat the corn kernels very dry - moisture prevents them from crisping properly.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the corn in a single layer.
Toast, stirring often, 8-10 minutes until deeply golden and crunchy throughout - this is cancha, a beloved crunchy snack found on every Peruvian table.
Season with cumin and a pinch of salt while still warm.

name: Spiced Peruvian Nuts
steps: Toss almonds with aji paste, toast in a dry skillet 3-4 minutes.
steps_detailed: Toss the almonds with aji amarillo paste and honey.
Toast in a dry skillet over medium-low heat, 4-5 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant and lightly caramelized.
Cool completely before eating - they crisp up as they cool.

name: Sweet Potato Chips (Peruvian)
steps: Toss sweet potato slices with oil, roast at 400F for 20-25 minutes until crisp.

ingredients_full.note: very thinly sliced, ideally with a mandoline

steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F. Slice the sweet potato as thin and evenly as possible for the crispiest result.
Toss with oil and cinnamon if using.
Spread in a single layer and roast 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until crisp and lightly browned at the edges.
Cool completely before serving - they crisp further as they cool.

name: Purple Corn Pudding (Mazamorra Morada Style)
steps: Simmer berries with 1.5 cups water 10 minutes, strain.
Whisk in cornstarch, cook until thickened, sweeten with honey, chill.
steps_detailed: Simmer the berries with 2 cups water and cinnamon for 10-12 minutes to extract their color and flavor - this stands in for the traditional purple corn, which gives mazamorra morada its signature deep purple color.
Strain out the solids, keeping the liquid.
Whisk the cornstarch into a splash of cold water, then whisk into the warm liquid.
Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, 5-6 minutes until thickened to a pudding consistency.
Stir in the honey, then chill at least 1 hour.
Top with diced pineapple before serving, a traditional garnish.

name: Alfajores-Style Bites
steps: Mix flour and butter into a shortbread dough, bake small rounds at 350F for 10-12 minutes.
Sandwich cooled cookies with dulce de leche.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 350F.
Cream the butter and sugar together, then mix in the vanilla.
Fold in the flour and cornstarch just until a soft dough forms - the cornstarch is what gives alfajores their signature melt-in-your-mouth texture.
Roll into small rounds, flatten slightly, and bake 10-12 minutes until just set but not browned.
Cool completely, then sandwich pairs of cookies together with dulce de leche.

name: Peruvian Shrimp and Rice
steps: Saute shrimp with aji paste, 4-5 minutes until pink and cooked through.
Serve over cooked rice.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the garlic, cooking 30 seconds.
Add the shrimp and aji amarillo paste, cooking 4-5 minutes until the shrimp are pink and just cooked through - don't overcook, shrimp turn rubbery fast.
Finish with lime juice.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve the shrimp over it, topped with cilantro.
